employer.title: Stage 1 · Register & get verified
employer.text: We begin by creating a new employer account on HireStream, the H P S E D C overseas placement portal. Issue number one from the test report asked us to clearly define employer verification. Because the employer is an overseas company, not an Indian one, the form captures their foreign company details and their country of operation, chosen from a list of overseas destinations. They then upload the documents H P S E D C requires from a foreign principal employer — the demand letter specifying the manpower request, a power of attorney authorising the Indian recruiting agent to act on their behalf, the business registration or trade licence, the employment contract, and the authorised signatory's passport. Once submitted, H P S E D C reviews and approves the company, and the verification status is shown to the employer throughout.

employer.title: Stage 2 · Post a requisition
employer.text: Once verified, the employer posts a hiring requisition. This profile is what the matching engine scores candidates against, so it is worth completing thoroughly. Under role basics they set the job title, the company, the destination country and city, and the overseas job category — issue number eight. Requisition details capture the number of positions, the minimum experience, the salary range, and how urgent the role is. The hiring criteria — the minimum qualification and the languages required, on the C E F R scale — feed directly into the match score, issue number two. Finally they add a job description and the key skills. Each of these — qualification, experience, language, skills, and category — becomes a weighted factor in every candidate's match percentage.

employer.title: Stage 3 · The applications pipeline
employer.text: After publishing, applications flow through a clear six-stage pipeline — submitted, reviewed, shortlisted, interview, selected, and placed. From the dashboard the employer sees, at a glance, how many candidates sit at each stage across all of their requisitions.

employer.title: Stage 4 · Review & schedule interviews
employer.text: Opening the pipeline, the employer reviews shortlisted candidates — each carrying a match score from the engine, issue number two. Interviews are usually arranged by the recruiting agency, but the employer can also schedule one directly here — choosing the date, time, mode, and interviewer — and the candidate is notified automatically.

employer.title: Stage 5 · Selection & placement
employer.text: The employer also monitors the later stages — interview, selection, and placement. While the recruiting agency drives the offer, visa assistance, and post-placement welfare, the employer can track every selected and placed candidate, and step in wherever their own decision is required.

employer.title: Stage 6 · Stay informed
employer.text: And throughout the whole journey, the notification centre keeps the employer informed at every step — new shortlists, interview confirmations, and status changes — so nothing is missed.

agent.title: Stage 1 · Agency registration & MEA documents
agent.text: We turn now to the recruitment agency. To address issue number three from the test report, a new agency registers on HireStream and submits its credentials for H P S E D C approval. As a licensed Indian recruiting agent it uploads the nine documents the Ministry of External Affairs mandates — the M E A recruiting licence, the certificate of incorporation, PAN and G S T, office address proof, the authorised signatory's I D, any labour or recruitment permission, proof of past overseas placement experience, and the recruitment undertaking with H P S E D C. The agency's verification status is tracked throughout.

agent.title: Stage 2 · Pick up a requisition
agent.text: Once H P S E D C verifies the agency, it can pick up employer requisitions. In Open Requisitions the agency sees every demand from verified employers, and picks one up — which creates a public job posting it now owns and sources candidates against.

agent.title: Stage 3 · Source & screen candidates
agent.text: As candidates apply, the agency manages them through the applications pipeline. Each applicant carries a match score from the engine — issue number two — and the agency screens, reviews, and shortlists the strongest candidates for the employer.

agent.title: Stage 4 · Schedule interviews
agent.text: For shortlisted candidates the agency schedules interviews — setting the date, time, mode, and interviewer — and the candidate is notified automatically. The agency coordinates the whole interview process on behalf of the employer.

agent.title: Stage 5 · Placement, visa & welfare
agent.text: After a candidate is selected, the agency drives the deployment. On the candidate's record it issues the appointment letter, tracks the visa and passport status, and records the mandatory thirty, sixty, and ninety day post-placement welfare check-ins — the agency's core responsibility under the Emigration Act.

candidate.title: Stage 1 · Sign up as a job seeker
candidate.text: Welcome to HireStream — the H P S E D C overseas placement portal, and your single window to verified jobs abroad. For a job seeker, the journey starts with a free account. You choose 'Job Seeker', enter your name, email and a password, and you're in. From here, HireStream guides you all the way, from building your profile to a safe placement overseas.

candidate.title: Stage 2 · Your personal & passport details
candidate.text: Your profile is the heart of HireStream, and it opens with your personal details. To address issue number four from the test report, the portal now captures everything the Ministry of External Affairs expects of an emigrant — your father's and mother's name, your current and permanent address, your passport number and its expiry, your E C R or non-E-C-R status, and your English proficiency as an I E L T S band. Entered once, these details travel with every application you make.

candidate.title: Stage 3 · Education & qualifications
candidate.text: Next is your education. Responding to issue number five, you can add as many qualifications as you hold — each with the degree, the institution, the year of passing, and your percentage or C G P A — from your tenth and twelfth, right up to your highest degree, diploma, or certification. The fuller your record, the stronger your matches.

candidate.title: Stage 4 · Work experience
candidate.text: Then your work experience — every role you've held, with the company, your position, the years, the country, and what you achieved. Following issue number six, we removed the old, misleading note that suggested only candidates with three or more years would match well. Every applicant is now matched fairly, on the complete picture of their profile.

candidate.title: Stage 5 · Your documents
candidate.text: The last section is your documents. Issue number seven asked us to separate these clearly — so your C V and your passport now have their own dedicated slots, alongside your identity proof and your educational and experience certificates. Each document that H P S E D C verifies carries a green tick, so you always know exactly what has been accepted.

candidate.title: Stage 6 · Find jobs & your match score
candidate.text: With your profile complete, you can discover jobs. Every vacancy from verified employers and agencies appears here — searchable, and filtered by destination country, category, experience and salary. And against each one, issue number two, the matching engine shows your personal match score: a single percentage that weighs your skills, experience, qualifications, preferred country, language and salary against what the role demands. Open any application to see how well you fit, and the exact skills the role is looking for.

candidate.title: Stage 7 · Apply & track your application
candidate.text: Applying takes a single click — and from then on, every application is tracked through a clear six-stage pipeline: submitted, reviewed, shortlisted, interview, selected, and placed. You always know precisely where you stand. And when an interview is arranged, you're in control — confirm your attendance, request a reschedule, or decline, right from your dashboard.

candidate.title: Stage 8 · Offer, visa & welfare
candidate.text: Finally, the moment that changes a life. Once you're selected and your offer is issued, you accept it here — and your agency-driven deployment begins. From your own dashboard you follow your visa and passport status, your pre-departure checklist, and the mandatory thirty, sixty and ninety day welfare check-ins that protect you while you work abroad. From sign-up to safe placement, HireStream stays with you at every step.

admin.title: Stage 1 · Verification gate
admin.text: Overseeing the entire platform is the H P S E D C authority console. Nothing moves until H P S E D C approves it. Here, the authority reviews and verifies every recruiting agency and every overseas employer — checking their licences and their documents — before they are ever allowed to operate. This single gate is what keeps unregulated players out of the system.

admin.title: Stage 2 · System-wide overview
admin.text: From the overview, H P S E D C sees the whole system at a glance — the number of open job vacancies, the candidates and the placements, and the full application funnel across every agency and every destination country. It is a live, bird's-eye view of overseas recruitment for the entire state.

admin.title: Stage 3 · Compliance & welfare
admin.text: The compliance and welfare tools track the duties that protect the migrant worker. The authority monitors the visa pipeline, flags any candidate placed before approvals are complete, and oversees the mandatory thirty, sixty, and ninety-day welfare check-ins that every agency must record after a placement — so that no worker is forgotten once they are abroad.

admin.title: Stage 4 · The audit trail
admin.text: And underpinning all of it is the audit log — an immutable, time-stamped record of every action on the platform: who did what, and when. For a government system, this is the backbone of accountability. Every decision can be traced, and nothing can be quietly changed.
